programme.title: Forbidden Science | Is It Real?
programme.desc: Forbidden Science is a unique blend of noir science fiction and erotic programming. Set in the near future, the stories revolve around the evil corporation, 4Ever Innovations, whose young and brilliant scientists download and sell memories. They replace those we've lost with clones and devise technology that helps us live out our greatest fantasies; from saving a failed relationship, to creating the perfect lover, to being irresistible to the opposite sex - but sometimes our deepest desires come at a terrible price. 4Ever offers customers the opportunity to live out their fantasies in a virtual world but there are secrets within 4Ever Innovations that may cost you your life.

programme.desc: The Odyssea Sportfishing family grows as Casey Villone comes aboard as Rush's new deck hand. While Rush gained a helping hand on his charters, he also scored a new "Fishing Mate" who is always game to bend a rod. With a day off and a report of Tuna offshore, the two head out to see if they can beat the sharks and land a Key West sushi dinner.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: Rush teams up with longtime friend and fellow angler Jesus Diaz for a well-earned "busman's holiday" in the beautiful waters of Key West. Even on a day off, these two can't resist seeing what's going to bite. Taking the wind and the tides into account, the duo maps out a strategic circuit through Key West's surrounding waters to target cobia, grouper, snapper, and tuna for a busy day "off" work.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: Water quality is everything—and the Star brite - SeaSafe team is back at it for the second year in a row! Led by Cory Redwine, Conservation Director of Project SeaSafe, this is the 4th Annual Clean Water Collective and it’s bigger than ever. This time, we’re in Grant, Florida, where 4 million clams are being released into the Indian River Lagoon, paired with the planting of thousands of mangroves, oyster work by CCA and trash clean ups to help restore one of Florida’s most critical ecosystems. But it’s not just about hard work—it’s about connection. With local captains, fishing legends, and the Local Knowledge crew on hand, we hit the water to show why this place is worth fighting for. From clam drops by drone, to in-water mangrove planting, to monster snook catches, this is hands-on conservation at its best. This two-part series kicks off with action, heart, and the kind of hope only a healthy waterway can bring. Don’t miss Part 1—where conservation meets community and everyone gets their hands wet.

programme.desc: One of the 7 Natural Wonders of the World, Victoria Falls is an amazing spectacle that straddles the Zambia/Zimbabwe border. The force of the water is so intense that the water bouncing back creates a rainforest. Remarkably, the wildlife in the area is as abundant as the water going over the falls.

programme.title: Water Matters Ep102: Ocean Impact
programme.desc: Water Matters documents water-related environmental issues from around the globe as a number of the world’s best documentary filmmakers and environmental organizations share their stories regarding the planet’s water crisis as well as potential solutions for a healthier world.

programme.title: Hot Water Ep108: Behind-The-Scenes of Unlimited Superboat Jet Sprinting
programme.desc: Hot Water goes behind-the-scenes of Unlimited Superboat Jet Sprinting. V8 Unlimited Superboats are the top racing class in Jet Sprinting with the boats being unrestricted.

programme.title: Water Ways TV Ep211: Nova Scotia, Canada
programme.desc: Host Steven Bull explores the history Nova Scotia with a to-the-centimetre replica of the “Hector”, checks out the oldest Yacht Club in the Americas and then goes Tidal Bore Rafting.

programme.title: Boom America
programme.desc: The episode opens with Michael Bellemare of Switch It Up Inc., who shares how a simple bar-napkin sketch grew into a million-dollar operation specializing in turnkey solar, LED lighting, and EV charging. With a passion for revitalizing the trades, Michael stresses the importance of training programs and investing in people as he maps out his goal of scaling to a $100M company—and even explores franchising with Kevin. Next, teacher-turned-inventor Sean Worthington introduces Raida Tech, where a bathtub question—“What is money?”—led to a new form of data-supremacy currency and safer, uncensored AI. Sean looks to the A-Team for marketing, sales, and customer acquisition support. Then, Dale Krueger of Tangible Assets LLC explains how his coin-collecting hobby evolved into an investment platform blending art and assets, now ready to scale beyond word-of-mouth investors. Finally, Richard Simon of New Home National Title showcases his on-call title insurance service and seeks guidance on breaking into TV advertising to reach homebuyers nationwide.

programme.title: Boom America
programme.desc: It’s overhaul day on Boom America, and the A-Team gets to work. Kevin Harrington and Toli Cefail kick things off with Switch It Up, laying out strategies around “The Volume” while Kyle Reyes pushes for a powerful pivot—refocusing the company squarely on the booming trades community. Next, the team digs into Raida Tech, where Laura highlights how the company is operating 25 years ahead of the curve but desperately needs clearer marketing to match its groundbreaking innovation. American Stories creator Bob Cefail stops by to share the inspiration behind Boom America and the future of independent production in the U.S. From there, the A-Team turns to Tangible Assets, helping refine how Dale can monetize his coin collection business, boost credibility, and reach the right investors. Finally, New Home National Title gets a full diagnostic from the experts as they map out how the company can scale, gain visibility, and earn the recognition it deserves nationwide.

programme.title: Farm Hero
programme.desc: This week, host Laura visits a family who has taken backyard farming to a whole new level. The Killpatrick family led by husband and father Michael is paving the way for the next generation of regenerative farming. This farm sprouted from the 2020 pandemic and in a few short years has become a pivotal cornerstone of their city community for all things holistic and organic. The Farm on Central is proving what can be accomplished with dedication to the future of farming.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Brace yourself for an edge-of-your-seat Tyrolean traverse (a horizontal ladder) over—and occasionally plunging into—the alligator occupied lake. Then, watch as our celebrity shooters and special guests unleash the power of some truly formidable machinery.

programme.title: The Big Reveal
programme.desc: Take flight with The Big Reveal as Don Mann (Surviving Mann), Sheriff Mark Lamb (Surviving Mann), and Laura Zaspel (Farm Hero) visit Air Tractor—America’s iconic maker of crop dusters, firefighting, and military aircraft. Witness the power, precision, and pride behind the company proving that American craftsmanship still soars.

programme.title: California Superbloom
programme.desc: Experience the awe inspiring explosion of wildflowers across the California landscape! Enjoy aerial views over multi colored hillsides, journey through wildflower meadows, and relax with an uplifting acoustic soundtrack. This film is an updated and expanded version of our 2017 Superbloom film, and contains new scenes from Californias amazing 2023 spring season. The full 2 hour "California Superbloom" is available to subscribers of NatureStream.tv

programme.title: Autumn Exhilaration Nature Film
programme.desc: The exhilaration of autumn in the Colorado Rockies and High Sierras awaits you in our latest film release! Soar over the brilliant aspen forests of Colorado, immerse yourself in the glowing autumn leaves, and bask in the golden light of sunrise on the high mountain peaks. The inspiring scenery is accompanied by a soothing ambient music soundtrack. Available to subscribers of NatureStream.tv. Runtime 2.5 Hours.

programme.title: Preview for "California's Wild Coast"
programme.desc: Soar with the seabirds over the wild and beautiful north coast of California. The rugged coastline of Mendocino is a vast wilderness full of wildlife, sea caves, bird colonies, and crashing waves that can only be fully appreciated from an aerial view. This 2.5 hour film is an exhilarating flight over this magnificent landscape. Soul stirring ambient music accompanies the spectacular cinematography of "Californias Wild Coast". The full film is available to subscribers of NatureStream.tv

programme.title: The Pacific Coast
programme.desc: Experience the natural beauty of the Pacific Coast from Washington State to Southern California. See breathtaking video of spectacular sunsets and iconic locations along the Big Sur coast. The photography and cinematography of Olof and Elizabeth Carmel are paired with relaxing music to provide an exquisite "ambient art" experience for TV and mobile devices.

programme.title: Preview of "Hawaii, A Journey Through The Big Island"
programme.desc: Experience Big Island bliss in the stunning 2 hour film, available for streaming on May 16. Soar above waterfalls that plunge into the Pacific, kick back with views of beach sunsets and slow motion waves, enjoy island palms in the gentle island breezes, and experience the Waipio valley paradise from above. programme.title: Trans-Saharan Highway | Brilliant Corners: Algeria, Episode 3
programme.desc: After heading inland for a whirlwind adventure in the Sahara Desert, the crew says farewell to Lucy as she heads home to Australia. Back in Algiers, Sam, Erwan, and Dihya visit one of the country's leading digital agencies to discuss their journey and go for one last surf in some perfect longboard waves. Before leaving the country, Sam and Erwan talk with local surfers Dihya and Walid about the future of surfing in Algeria and how the emergence of the sport could impact the people there.
